CTK87: Over a thousand of these have gone through our shop — the Workwear Pocket Tee is the Carhartt piece crews order by the case. Heavyweight cotton, a real chest pocket, and the loose original fit that layers over anything. We print or embroider the company mark over the pocket and it reads like the uniform it becomes.
CT105291: The Force Solid Long Sleeve Shirt is Carhartt's modern button-up: their fast-drying Force fabric in a shirt clean enough for the front of the shop and tough enough for the back. Two hundred-plus have left our shop with embroidered logos — field-service and equipment companies are the usual buyers.
CT105292: The short-sleeve cut of Carhartt's Force Solid work shirt — the quick-drying button-up that replaced sweat-soaked cotton uniforms for a lot of our service-company customers. Sixty-plus orders and counting; left-chest logo, name on the right, classic shop-shirt layout.
CT104616: The Force Short Sleeve Pocket Tee is the performance version of the classic pocket tee — Carhartt's sweat-wicking, fast-drying fabric in the silhouette crews already trust. Made for summer job sites; 250-plus have gone out under our printers and embroidery heads.
CT102538: The Rugged Professional Series Long Sleeve is the Carhartt shirt built for the customer-facing side of trade work — polished enough for estimates and inspections, tough enough for the yard. Companies uniform their whole front line in it with an embroidered chest logo.
CT102537: The short-sleeve Rugged Professional is the summer half of Carhartt's dressed-up work shirt line — clean collar, work-grade build. It carries one of the strongest margins in our Carhartt program, and customers who try one shirt tend to come back for the crew.
CT104255: The Force 1/4-Zip Long Sleeve Tee splits the difference between a work shirt and athletic gear — Carhartt's fast-dry fabric with a zip collar that vents when the work heats up. A hundred-plus decorated here; it embroiders cleanly at the left chest.
CT106652: The current generation of Carhartt's Force Short Sleeve Pocket Tee — same fast-drying workwear formula, refreshed fit and fabric. It's taking over where its predecessor left off; crews that ran the old one reorder this without blinking.
CTTK87: The tall cut of the thousand-plus-selling Workwear Pocket Tee — full length in the body and sleeves so it stays tucked and covered on tall frames. Ordered alongside the regular CTK87, printed identically.
CT107107: The Force Sun Defender Short Sleeve Shirt adds sun protection to Carhartt's fast-drying work fabric — a button-up built for full days in open sun. Landscape, marine, and utility crews are the natural fit; the chest takes a clean embroidered mark.
CT107109: The Rugged Flex Plaid Long Sleeve is the flannel-look Carhartt with stretch built into the weave — 38 orders in its first stretch with us, almost all singles and pairs, which tells you it's the shirt people pick for themselves. A quiet embroidered logo on the chest keeps it company wear.
CT104617: The long-sleeve Force Pocket Tee carries Carhartt's fast-dry fabric into shoulder-season coverage — the tee for crews that work hard enough to sweat in October. Pocket-side decoration keeps the workwear look intact.
CT106660: Carhartt's current Force 1/4-Zip Long Sleeve — the vented, fast-drying pullover tee, updated. It slots into uniform programs as the athletic option next to the classic pocket tees, one logo across all of them.
CT106020: The straightforward Carhartt Short Sleeve T-Shirt — the label and the build without the pocket. It's the value door into a Carhartt tee program, and it takes a bold printed chest or back graphic better than the pocket styles.
CTK128: The Long Sleeve Henley is the Carhartt tee dressed up a notch — three buttons, no collar, still workwear to the bone. Twenty-five separate orders tell us it's a keeper piece; crews wear it off the clock, which is exactly what you want a logo doing.
CT106923: The Force Sun Defender Long Sleeve Hooded Tee is sun protection you can actually work in — light, fast-drying, with a hood for the back of the neck. It's become standard issue for landscaping and marine crews in the sunny half of the year; a printed logo keeps it crew gear.
CTK84: The Short Sleeve Henley brings the three-button collar-free look to summer weight — a Carhartt tee that doesn't read as a uniform until you add the embroidered logo. Small crews and owner-operators favor it.
CT106921: Carhartt's current Long Sleeve T-Shirt — the everyday heavyweight LS without the pocket, at the value end of the line. Fifteen separate orders in short order; it prints big and clean, front or back.
CT106868: The Force Sun Defender Short Sleeve Tee is the lightest sun-protective piece in the line — Carhartt's fast-dry fabric with sun protection for full-exposure days. Print it light-handed and it stays as breathable as it came.
CT106656: The current Force Long Sleeve Pocket Tee — Carhartt's fast-drying pocket tee with coverage, in the refreshed cut. Ten orders in ten different batches says crews are finding it one at a time and coming back.
CT106972: The Force Sun Defender Long Sleeve Tee covers arms against sun without cooking the wearer — the middle option between the short sleeve and the hooded version. Utility and highway crews order it into summer programs.
CT107106: The Force Sun Defender Long Sleeve Shirt is the button-up of Carhartt's sun-protection line — collar and coverage for all-day exposure, in fabric that dries before break is over. Embroiders like any of the Force shirts.
CT106688: The current-generation Rugged Professional Short Sleeve — Carhartt's clean-collar work shirt, updated. Companies moving from the original CT102537 keep the same logo layout and nobody notices the changeover.
CT106689: The current Rugged Professional Long Sleeve — the dressed-up Carhartt work shirt in its refreshed cut. A dozen orders already; it holds the same estimate-to-yard versatility the line is known for.
CT106880: The Force Snag-Resistant Pocket Polo puts Carhartt's name on a true uniform polo — snag-resistant knit, chest pocket, fast-dry fabric. It's the polo for companies whose crews are too hard on standard pique. Embroiders sharp on the chest.
CT106899: The Force Sun Defender Polo combines Carhartt's fast-dry fabric with sun protection in a collared cut — the polo for outdoor service teams who need to look uniformed at the door and survive the afternoon in the open.
CTC16119: Carhartt's professional polo from the FLD & RSCU collection — a uniform-grade performance polo with the Carhartt name where a generic brand usually sits. Built for teams that live in polos; the knit takes an embroidered logo cleanly.
CTC12119: The women's cut of Carhartt's FLD & RSCU professional polo — the same uniform-grade knit in a tailored fit, so a Carhartt polo program covers the whole team properly.
CTC16129: The long-sleeve version of Carhartt's FLD & RSCU polo — collar and coverage for uniformed teams working the cold half of the year. Pairs with the short sleeve in one program, one logo.
CTC86912: Carhartt's FLD & RSCU 1/2-Zip Mid-Layer — the uniform-program layering piece that goes over the matching polos. Zip collar, clean chest panel for embroidery, and the label crews respect.
CTC82912: The women's FLD & RSCU 1/2-Zip Mid-Layer — the tailored cut of Carhartt's uniform layering piece, completing the program for mixed teams.
CT102482: The Force Women's Midweight Utility Legging is workwear-grade base layering with real pockets — Carhartt's answer for women who found nothing between fashion leggings and men's thermals. A niche piece we stock for complete women's crew programs.
CT103574: The Rugged Flex Rigby Cargo Pant is Carhartt's stretch-fabric work pant with cargo utility — job-site tough without the stiff break-in. Crews add pants to apparel orders so the whole kit ships together; sizing runs waist and inseam like denim.
CTB11: The Washed-Duck Work Dungaree is original-recipe Carhartt: pre-washed duck canvas legwear that outlasts everything else in the order. Companies add them to full-uniform programs; twenty-five pairs at a time is the usual pattern.
CT102291: The Rugged Flex Rigby Pant is the straight work pant of Carhartt's stretch line — cleaner than the cargo, tougher than office khakis. Stocked for uniform programs that want pants under the same label as the jackets.
CT103542: The cargo short of Carhartt's Rugged Flex line — stretch-fabric utility for the two months a Northwest crew genuinely wants shorts, and the warmer trades that want them year-round.
CT102804: Carhartt's Rugged Flex 5-Pocket Jean — a work jean with stretch built in, for crews that live in denim. Part of our full-uniform stocking so pants ship with the logoed tops.
CT102808: The Rugged Flex Utility Jean adds work-specific pocketing to Carhartt's stretch denim — the jean for tradespeople who carry tools in their pants whether they meant to or not.
CT106672: Carhartt's current Firm Duck Insulated Bib Overalls — the full-coverage cold-weather workhorse in stiff, protective firm duck with real insulation. The piece Northwest crews order when the job doesn't stop for winter.
CT104393: The Firm Duck Insulated Bib Overalls in the classic generation — heavy, warm, and nearly indestructible. Outdoor winter trades order these with the jackets and wear them until spring.
CT102776: The Duck Unlined Bib Overalls are the three-season version of Carhartt's iconic bibs — full coverage and duck-canvas protection without the insulation bulk. Painters, concrete crews, and fabricators are the regulars.
CTS104393: The short-inseam cut of Carhartt's Firm Duck Insulated Bibs — proper proportions for shorter frames so the crotch and knees land where they should. Same warmth, same toughness.
CTS106672: The short cut of the current-generation Firm Duck Insulated Bibs — full winter protection, proportioned right for shorter crew members.
CTT104393: The tall cut of the classic Firm Duck Insulated Bibs — length in the leg and rise for tall frames working through winter.
CTT106672: The tall version of Carhartt's current Firm Duck Insulated Bibs — cold-weather coverage that actually reaches the boots on a 6'4" frame.
